# Defect Ledger

| ID | Gate | Finding | Root cause | Disposition | Regression evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| D-001 | 4 | Serialized provider lock failed when its parent did not exist | lock assumed precreated directory | fixed: lock creates private parent | storage lock tests |
| D-002 | 4 | degraded final ignored publication failure | error discarded in terminal helper | fixed: mandatory error propagation | application synthetic run |
| D-003 | 5 | first-pass prompt omitted required reviewer ID | semantic contract not represented in prompt | fixed: explicit bound ID | CLI local HTTP E2E |
| D-004 | 5 | Claude JSON envelope reached schema validator intact | adapter returned transport envelope | fixed: bounded structured-output unwrap | adapter regression test |
| D-005 | 4 | lifecycle omitted clustered/consensus/recommended states and active pointer | initial orchestration persisted only major phases | fixed: complete journal sequence and pointers | application artifact assertions |
| D-006 | 6 | top-level symlink input was accepted after canonicalization | `Lstat` occurred on resolved target | fixed: inspect requested path before resolution and again after read | document symlink regression |
| D-007 | 6 | injected HTTP client bypassed worker redirect policy | redirect callback installed only on default client | fixed: clone every client, enforce redirect target and public-IP dialing | redirect escape regression |
| D-008 | 6 | panel weights were clamped on a copy and never enabled in consensus | validation/value semantics mismatch | fixed: pointer normalization and weighted resolution | domain weight table |
| D-009 | 6 | same-millisecond ULIDs could collide | raw entropy used for each ID | fixed: locked monotonic entropy | consecutive fixed-clock ULID test |
| D-010 | 6 | arbitration/edit/revert lacked run locks and default editing selected disabled Claude | mutation paths assumed CLI serialization | fixed: run revalidation/locks and capable editor selection | app edit and lock suites |
| D-011 | 6 | blind packets retained reviewer-derived finding IDs | identity fields removed but IDs left intact | fixed: deterministic opaque ballot IDs and host mapping | blind-packet regression |
| D-012 | 6 | TOML/user JSON accepted unknown fields | decoders did not inspect undecoded keys/strict recovery | fixed: fail-closed config, persona, arbitration, and proposal decoding | config/contract tests |
| D-013 | 6 | edit apply did not reread immediately before write; multi-file revert lacked rollback | validation and mutation separated by a race window | fixed: pre-write hash check and revert rollback | edit stale/user-change suite |
| D-014 | 6 | lock and JSONL paths could follow pre-existing symlinks | append/open calls lacked no-follow policy | fixed: `O_NOFOLLOW` locks and non-regular journal rejection | external sentinel regression |
| D-015 | 6 | deterministic chunk map was not part of packet identity | hash computed before split projection | fixed: manifest/chunks/redactions in projection and rehash after split | packet identity regression |
| D-016 | 7 | Codex refused the external run directory because it was not a Git worktree | adapter omitted the documented non-repository execution flag | fixed: pass `--skip-git-repo-check` with the external call directory | argv regression + authenticated smoke |
| D-017 | 7 | Agy rejected the numeric print timeout | adapter serialized seconds while Agy requires a Go duration | fixed: serialize the bounded duration with a unit | argv regression + authenticated smoke |
| D-018 | 7 | Codex rejected response schemas whose `const`/`enum` properties omitted explicit types | local validator accepted a broader JSON Schema surface than the provider | fixed: separate strict provider schemas from tolerant bounded readers; assert provider subset recursively | schema regression + authenticated smoke |
| D-019 | repair 1 | blind voters lacked the frozen document, rubric, and verification evidence | the vote boundary passed only anonymous findings and a packet hash | fixed: versioned identical blind vote packets and complete delivery attestations | app synthetic ballot equality and content assertions |
| D-020 | repair 1 | successful retrieval was promoted to semantic worker verification | transport provenance and claim support shared one boolean | fixed fail-closed: retrieved evidence remains explicitly unverified; model cannot self-promote | injected successful unrelated-evidence regression |
| D-021 | repair 1 | token budget was preflight-only and actual provider usage was discarded | invocation boundary had no durable reservation/accounting ledger | fixed: per-call durable reservation/reconciliation and provider output caps | budget exhaustion, OpenAI payload, race tests |
| D-022 | repair 1 | unevidenced severity cap applied only to factual claims | global rule nested inside the factual-claim branch | fixed: category-independent severity normalization | every category × unevidenced blocker regression |
| D-023 | repair 2 | resume repeated completed provider calls and replaced same-path artifacts | recovery re-entered the full review use case | fixed: strict checkpoint reducer reuses completed review/vote artifacts and invokes only absent work | no-duplicate-call resume regression after removing terminal artifacts |
| D-024 | repair 2 | persisted packet identity was trusted without recomputation | stored hash fields were treated as proof of decoded content | fixed: centralized packet invariant, path, nested-version, item/rubric, and canonical-hash validation | packet field-tampering matrix and corrupt-resume refusal |
| D-025 | repair 2 | persisted readers accepted unknown nested schema versions and fields | permissive JSON decode plus outer-only checks | fixed: closed JSON decoding and recursive domain/artifact validation at trust re-entry | nested finding version corruption and strict checkpoint tests |
| D-026 | repair 2 | workspace ledger changed before terminal publication completed | mutable projection was written before report/state/final | fixed: durable publication intent/candidate; report and terminal commit precede idempotent workspace projections | resume publication recovery and full application gate |
| D-027 | repair 3 | document edit/revert writes could outlive failed or crashed lifecycle publication | file-level atomic writes lacked a durable multi-artifact transaction protocol | fixed: prepare/apply/commit transaction journal, per-file recovery hashes, rollback, terminal reconciliation, and manual hold | interrupted-source recovery, terminal-match commit, revert/user-change, race tests |
| D-028 | repair 4 | release archive smoke ran only after GitHub Release publication | GoReleaser's build and publish phases were coupled in the predecessor job | fixed: signed candidates are built without publishing, smoke-tested as exact workflow artifacts, then attested and published | static build→smoke→publish workflow assertion and YAML parse |
| D-029 | repair 5 | architecture intent, ownership, invariants, exceptions, and baseline were prose-only | no scope-bearing machine registry or conformance gate existed | fixed: versioned `.architecture` registry, dependency validator, expiry enforcement, baseline accounting, and standard-gate integration | repository conformance plus deliberate forbidden-edge and expired-exception fixtures |
| D-030 | repair 2026-07-22 | resume mutated run state without the run lock | recovery branches predated lock discipline | fixed: bounded run-lock acquisition and revalidation before mutation | flock-contention resume regression |
| D-031 | repair 2026-07-22 | accept-majority rejected disputes whose default carried a prior ruling | decision memory overwrote the parsed default text | fixed: memory_hint field, legacy defaults honored | panel-default and legacy-ruling tests |
| D-032 | repair 2026-07-22 | failed --json commands printed a zero-value final | rendering ran before the error path returned | fixed: error envelope plus zero-final guard and RunE decorator | CLI contract tests |
| D-033 | repair 2026-07-22 | interactive arbitrate with no final exited 0 silently | nil error returned for absent final | fixed: explicit preflight error | code path reasoned; TTY-only |
| D-034 | repair 2026-07-22 | model-declared worker-verified evidence survived --no-workers | downgrade lived only in the skippable verification phase | fixed: host-side sanitization in validatePass | validatePass downgrade test |
| D-035 | repair 2026-07-22 | replay and edit rereview of split runs always failed preflight | Split flag not re-derived from persisted chunks | fixed: chunk-derived split passthrough | split replay regression |
| D-036 | repair 2026-07-22 | agy prompt as one argv element hit exec limits opaquely | agy CLI has no stdin/file prompt mode | partial: platform-aware pre-exec cap with honest remedy; stdin delivery blocked upstream | oversized-prompt argv test |
| D-037 | repair 2026-07-22 | OpenAI-compatible key exported to every provider subprocess | secrets injected into the allowlisted child env | fixed: env allowlist only; secrets redaction-only | restrictedEnv secret test |
| D-038 | repair 2026-07-22 | DOCX/PDF hash and content came from two file reads | extraction re-opened the source path | fixed: single-read extraction (zip over bytes; private PDF copy) | extraction tests |
| D-039 | repair 2026-07-22 | anchors bound first occurrence; fuzzy ambiguity check dead | no uniqueness requirement | fixed: unique binding with contextual disambiguation | anchor ambiguity tests |
| D-040 | repair 2026-07-22 | ledger stale-record loop was a no-op | disposition never implemented | fixed: scope-aware staleness with sticky dispositions | in/out-of-scope staleness tests |
| D-041 | repair 2026-07-22 | leftover recovery backup blocked edit retry forever | existence check had no legitimate-retry path | fixed: identical-content reuse, content-addressed archive | rollback-retry tests |
| D-042 | repair 2026-07-22 | torn journal tails bricked decisions and transcript | readers fatal on any undecodable line; appends never repaired | fixed: quarantine-and-truncate on append, tolerant readers | torn-tail recovery test |
| D-043 | repair 2026-07-22 | CI lacked race detection and vulnerability scanning | gate soft-skipped govulncheck; no -race job | fixed: race job, govulncheck required in CI | workflow inspection |
| D-044 | repair 2026-07-22 | docs claimed panel files, custom rubrics, per-hunk confirmation | documentation drift | fixed: SPEC/io-contract aligned to implementation | doc diff |
| D-045 | repair 2026-07-22 | consensus tie used exact float equality | float accumulation of weights | fixed: integer-hundredth weights, NaN-proof clamp | exact weighted-tie test |
| D-046 | repair 2026-07-22 | 32-bit fingerprints keyed ledger and decision memory | truncated hash chosen for display reused as identity | fixed: 16-hex fingerprints; legacy stores fail closed | fingerprint and compat tests |
| D-047 | repair 2026-07-22 | one-character cluster ID panicked arbitration ranking | unvalidated persisted ID sliced by prefix | fixed: cluster ID shape validation, TrimPrefix | malformed-ID tests |
| D-048 | repair 2026-07-22 | sub-second call timeout became the 15-minute default | int truncation at the adapter boundary | fixed: 1s minimum validated at normalize | config limits tests |
| D-049 | repair 2026-07-22 | recommend --kind was silently dead | --rubric default always won | fixed: empty default, mutual exclusion | CLI flag tests |
| D-050 | repair 2026-07-22 | transcript printed local time with literal Z | wrong time layout | fixed: UTC RFC3339 | code diff |
| D-051 | repair 2026-07-22 | runtime faults exited as invalid arguments | unclassified errors defaulted to 4 | fixed: ExitInternal=7 at fault sites; parse errors keep 4 | exit-code tests |
| D-052 | repair 2026-07-22 | worker findings were uncapped | no per-worker bound | fixed: 100-finding cap per worker | spellcheck cap test |
| D-053 | repair 2026-07-22 | empty choices wrapped a nil error | two conditions shared one branch | fixed: distinct no-choices error | empty-choices test |
| D-054 | repair 2026-07-22 | output files read unbounded; read errors silently ignored | fallback conflated absence with failure | fixed: handle-checked bounded read; faults surface | code diff + suite |
| D-055 | repair 2026-07-22 | hand-rolled kill/wait raced pid reuse and hung on escaped pipes | select bypassed exec's cancel sequencing | fixed: cmd.Cancel group kill, WaitDelay, ErrWaitDelay tolerated | race suite + reasoning |
| D-056 | repair 2026-07-22 | doctor version probes unbounded | CombinedOutput with no deadline | fixed: 10s timeout, 64KiB cap, WaitDelay | code diff |
| D-057 | repair 2026-07-22 | injected HTTP clients followed cross-origin redirects | guard only on the fallback client | fixed: guard overlaid on shallow clone | redirect regression test |
| D-058 | repair 2026-07-22 | negative verification/arbitration caps silently disabled pipelines | unvalidated limits | fixed: minimum 1 validated | config limits tests |
| D-059 | repair 2026-07-22 | persona names joined into paths without slug check | validation lived only in one caller | fixed: slug check in ResolvePersona | traversal test |
| D-060 | repair 2026-07-22 | Transition accepted an invalid next state | only previous state validated | fixed: validate next before persisting | transition test |
| D-061 | repair 2026-07-22 | journal append and lock status could follow symlinks | opens lacked O_NOFOLLOW | fixed: no-follow opens | code diff + suite |
| D-062 | repair 2026-07-22 | case-variant paths defeated the state/document guard on darwin | byte-exact containment | fixed: case-folded containment (normalization residual documented) | case-variant test |
| D-063 | repair 2026-07-22 | raw reads and pdftotext stderr unbounded; DOCX cap hardcoded | caps applied inconsistently | fixed: raw cap, extraction caps honor the knob, stderr truncated | cap tests |
| D-064 | repair 2026-07-22 | duplicate DOCX entries enabled review/render divergence | first-entry-wins extraction | fixed: normalized duplicate rejection | duplicate-entry test |
| D-065 | repair 2026-07-22 | chunk IDs scrambled past 9999 chunks | four-digit lexicographic IDs | fixed: eight-digit IDs | chunk order test |
| D-066 | repair 2026-07-22 | revert misreported crash rollback as user changes | record validated before recovery ran | fixed: recovery before record validation | crash-rollback revert test |
| D-067 | repair 2026-07-22 | aborted finals dropped worker findings and reason codes | abort path finalized before merge | fixed: merge and canonicalize before every finalization | code diff + suite |
| D-068 | repair 2026-07-22 | check.sh mutated the tree; release re-runs failed; dead goreleaser changelog | tooling drift | fixed: tidy -diff, idempotent publish, changelog disabled | workflow test + gate |
| D-069 | repair 2026-07-22 | ARCHITECTURE module table named absent symbols | doc drift | fixed: table matches exported surfaces | doc diff |
| D-070 | playtest 2026-07-23 | arbitration `Default` recommendation text could read "accept majority" on a genuine weighted tie or non-unanimous strict/configured-unanimity decision, letting `--accept-majority` silently auto-accept a dispute the panel never actually leaned toward accepting | `defaultRecommendation` derived text from raw unweighted `Accepts`/`Rejects` counts, which can diverge from the weighted vote comparison under non-uniform panel weights, across three separate `Reason` branches (`vote_tie`, `category_requires_full_panel_unanimity`, `unanimity_not_reached`) | fixed: new `Decision.WeightedLean` field computed once from the weighted sums before the outcome switch runs, correct for every `Reason`; `defaultRecommendation` reads only `WeightedLean`; `ValidateDecision` rejects decisions missing it | 3 named regression tests (vote_tie, strict-category, configured-unanimity) plus 51-scenario consensus playtest suite; two rounds of adversarial review |
| D-071 | scenario campaign 2026-07-22 | panel parsing rejected model identifiers with a trailing non-persona `@` suffix | every final `@` was treated as a persona delimiter even when the suffix did not match the documented persona slug grammar | fixed: only a valid trailing persona slug is stripped; all other `@` text remains part of the verbatim model identifier | panel parser regression test |
| D-072 | scenario campaign 2026-07-22 | diversity disclosure varied between duplicate model families | map iteration selected the first duplicate family without a stable order, producing nondeterministic reports/meta | fixed: duplicate family names are sorted before selecting the disclosure | repeated deterministic diversity-note test |
| D-073 | scenario campaign 2026-07-22 | a panel could carry a `NaN` weight past validation | clamp comparisons do not match `NaN`; later JSON persistence then fails with a generic error | fixed: panel normalization rejects non-finite weights before the run starts | panel normalization regression test |
